assertion_results.text: Each non-documentation, non-testing task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates doc-only scope) and testing tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates cross-cutting testing scope) are exempt from requiring Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes — they must still include Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Implementation tasks 1-5 each contain all required sections: Task 1 has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify, Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 2 has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 3 has all sections including Files to Modify and Files to Create. Task 4 has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 5 has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation task 6 (task-6-documentation.md) has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements and correctly omits Files to Modify, Files to Create, Implementation Notes. Testing tasks 7-8 have Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements and correctly omit Files to Modify, Files to Create, Implementation Notes.

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 12 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: 8 task files exist: task-1-advisory-severity-summary-model.md through task-8-performance-benchmarks.md. 8 is within the range [3, 12].
All 8 task files contain '## Repository' followed by 'trustify-backend': task-1 line 2, task-2 line 2, task-3 line 2, task-4 line 2, task-5 line 2, task-6 line 2, task-7 line 2, task-8 line 2.

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: From impact-map.md and task files: Task 1 has no dependencies. Task 2 depends on Task 1. Task 3 depends on Task 2. Task 4 depends on Task 3. Task 5 depends on Task 3. Task 6 depends on Task 3. Task 7 depends on Tasks 1-5. Task 8 depends on Tasks 1-5. This is a directed acyclic graph — all dependency edges point forward (lower-numbered to higher-numbered tasks) with no cycles.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: Files to Modify all exist in repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (Task 1), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (Task 2), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (Task 3), modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (Task 4). Files to Create are in existing directories from repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs (Task 1, in model/ dir), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (Task 3, in endpoints/ dir), tests/api/sbom_advisory_summary.rs (Task 5, in tests/api/ dir).

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map specifies concrete changes: New Files table lists exact paths with purposes (e.g., 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs — AdvisorySeveritySummary response struct'). Modified Files table specifies exact changes (e.g., 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs — Add advisory_summary method to SbomService'). API Changes table specifies 'GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary — NEW: Returns aggregated severity counts (critical, high, medium, low, total)'. Modules Affected lists specific impacts per module.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 tasks contain '## Repository' with the single value 'trustify-backend'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 tasks contain '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All modified file paths exist in repo-backend.md directory tree: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (line 41), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (line 46), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (line 48), modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (line 83). Created file paths are in directories that exist in repo-backend.md: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/ (line 40-43), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/ (line 48-50), tests/api/ (line 111-113).

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-documentation, non-testing task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance. Documentation tasks and testing tasks are exempt from this requirement as they intentionally omit Implementation Notes
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 Implementation Notes reference 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs', 'SbomSummary', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs', specific derives (Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug). Task 2 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs', 'entity/src/sbom.rs', 'entity/src/sbom_advisory.rs', 'entity/src/advisory.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs', 'common/src/error.rs', SeaORM group_by and count. Task 3 references 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs', 'common/src/error.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs', tower-http caching. Task 4 references 'modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs', 'entity/src/sbom_advisory.rs', 'modules/ingestor/src/service/mod.rs'. Task 5 references 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs', 'modules/ingestor/src/service/mod.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_summary.rs'. Tasks 6-8 are documentation/testing and exempt.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 tasks contain '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': task-1 line 5, task-2 line 5, task-3 line 5, task-4 line 5, task-5 line 5, task-6 line 5, task-7 line 5, task-8 line 5.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: 8 digest files exist (task-1-digest.md through task-8-digest.md), each containing '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-hex-chars>'. Verified hashes: task-1: sha256-md:05095de472cb28eaa0caa25e94f6e5fff2e0b9a697b2eb00dc5dd05a555dcf17, task-2: sha256-md:f17847a9de7f9fb6a9b96475a8cb7287a7e40db069ebcd1a5a4a239eae9e6894, task-3: sha256-md:24aa69583b99f8aa0b4733904c138e256cdbddc86b68a5934a3517c924b5bb40, task-4: sha256-md:a46d9af8623c5cc14a1820b0448f3a77b50b2608ea41652e55a51113e8b75091, task-5: sha256-md:c7c88bc125be11a188223488f47e8c9f2b766b604aa1f4180f377f3109d9626a, task-6: sha256-md:4cea257998a774686fe81b0996d1a1b7fc46c013dd6ba4b4a0294e926041e0ae, task-7: sha256-md:3e27c1e5cc9bbaf0d65719310fa5af469e5a0561bb260b4a720c50d0974a0a83, task-8: sha256-md:20d3f84bed219976c4f331465462bbd1106a2c552da2c2f6f999c728f45ef5d5. All are exactly 64 lowercase hex chars with sha256-md: prefix.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No task file contains any convention applicability rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'). None of the Implementation Notes sections in tasks 1-5 contain 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §' references or 'Applies:' rationale lines. The repo-backend.md directory tree includes a CONVENTIONS.md file at the repo root, and the tasks modify .rs files, so convention-aware enrichment should have produced rationales for applicable conventions. There is no evidence that convention-applicability-rules.md was followed.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: Impact-map.md states under 'Inherited Field Values': 'Priority: Major'. Summary-comment.md states under 'Field Propagation': 'Priority: Major' and shows JSON: '"priority": { "name": "Major" }'. The priority 'Major' (not 'Undefined') is documented as inherited by all 8 tasks.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: Impact-map.md states under 'Inherited Field Values': 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0'. Summary-comment.md states under 'Field Propagation': 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0' and shows JSON: '"fixVersions": [{ "name": "RHTPA 1.5.0" }]'. No fixVersion scope config exists in CLAUDE.md (### Jira Field Defaults is absent), so the default 'both' applies, meaning fixVersions should be inherited by tasks. This is correctly documented.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists and contains a 'Field Propagation' section: 'Priority: Major' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0', plus a JSON block showing the additional_fields. Additionally, impact-map.md contains an 'Inherited Field Values' section documenting the same values.

assertion_results.text: A documentation task is generated because the feature description's Documentation Considerations section indicates doc impact (Updates — add endpoint to REST API reference). The documentation task includes Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, and Dependencies sections, and omits Files to Modify, Files to Create, API Changes, Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, and Verification Commands
assertion_results.evidence: Task 6 (task-6-documentation.md) is titled 'Update REST API reference documentation'. It includes: Repository ('trustify-backend'), Target Branch ('main'), Description (about updating REST API reference for the new endpoint), Acceptance Criteria (6 checkboxes), Test Requirements (2 checkboxes), Dependencies ('Depends on: Task 3'). It correctly omits: Files to Modify, Files to Create, API Changes, Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, and Verification Commands — none of these section headers appear in the file.

assertion_results.text: Testing tasks are generated from the testing readiness template — exactly 2 testing tasks are created (one per test category: Smoke Tests and Performance Benchmarks). Each testing task includes Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, and Dependencies sections, and omits Files to Modify, Files to Create, API Changes, Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, and Verification Commands
assertion_results.evidence: Exactly 2 testing tasks exist: Task 7 (task-7-smoke-tests.md, titled 'Smoke tests') and Task 8 (task-8-performance-benchmarks.md, titled 'Performance benchmarks'). Both include: Repository ('trustify-backend'), Target Branch ('main'), Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Dependencies. Both omit: Files to Modify, Files to Create, API Changes, Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, Verification Commands — none of these section headers appear in either file.

assertion_results.text: Each testing task depends on all implementation tasks (not on documentation or other testing tasks)
assertion_results.evidence: Task 7 (smoke tests) Dependencies section lists: Task 1, Task 2, Task 3, Task 4, Task 5 — all 5 implementation tasks. Task 8 (performance benchmarks) Dependencies section lists: Task 1, Task 2, Task 3, Task 4, Task 5 — all 5 implementation tasks. Neither testing task depends on Task 6 (documentation) or the other testing task. Impact map confirms: 'Tasks 1-5 (all implementation) -> Task 7 (smoke tests), Task 8 (performance benchmarks)'.
